name: Pool Carve
history: Flowing through a bowl or pool using continuous carving turns. The most fundamental skill in pool and bowl skateboarding, dating to the earliest skatepark era of the 1970s.
youtube_query: pool carve skateboard bowl skating technique

name: Frontside Grind (Pool)
history: A 50-50 or 5-0 grind performed frontside on pool coping. Grinding pool coping is a defining skill of bowl skateboarding.

name: Backside Grind (Pool)
history: A grind on pool coping approached from the backside. The original backside grinds were pioneered by pool skaters in the late 1970s.
youtube_query: backside grind pool skateboard bowl tutorial

name: Poolside Ollie
history: An ollie above the pool coping, clearing the lip and returning to the pool. A fundamental aerial skill in pool and bowl skating.
youtube_query: ollie above lip pool skateboard bowl

name: Bowl Slash Grind
history: A carving grind where the back truck slashes across the coping at an angle. A classic aggressive bowl-skating grind.

name: Coping Grind
history: A general grind on the rounded or square coping that tops a pool or bowl. The coping of a pool was the original grinding obstacle in skateboarding.
youtube_query: coping grind skateboard bowl pool tutorial

name: Lip Trick
history: A broad category of tricks performed on or above the coping lip of a pool or bowl. Includes stalls, grinds, and slides on pool coping.
youtube_query: lip tricks bowl skateboard pool tutorial

name: Egg Bowl
history: Crouching into a tight ball while carving a pool or bowl to gain speed and create a dramatic visual. Inspired by egg-crouch techniques from snowboarding.
youtube_query: egg carve bowl pool skateboard technique

name: Frontside Nosegrind (Bowl)
history: A nosegrind on bowl coping approached frontside. Adapts the street nosegrind to pool/bowl coping.

name: Backside Smith (Bowl)
history: A Smith grind on pool coping approached from the backside. Requires aggressive commitment to lock the back truck below the coping.
youtube_query: backside smith grind pool bowl skateboard

name: Tuck Knee Invert
history: A handplant invert where one knee is tucked into the chest while inverted. A classic pool trick with strong visual impact.
youtube_query: tuck knee invert skateboard pool bowl

name: Pool Ollie North
history: An ollie over the lip of a pool with the front foot kicked forward off the nose while airborne. A stylish extension of the ollie north in pool context.

name: Pool 50-50 Revert
history: A 50-50 grind on pool coping that exits with a 180-degree revert. A smooth way to change direction after a grind.

name: Alley-oop Frontside Air
history: A frontside air performed in the alley-oop direction (against the natural rotation direction). A challenging and stylish pool trick.
youtube_query: alley oop frontside air pool skateboard

name: Full Pipe Run
history: Skating through a fully enclosed cylindrical concrete pipe, performing tricks on the walls and ceiling. A unique skateboarding format from the early 1970s.

name: Boneless
history: The front foot is grabbed and planted on the ground while the board is held, then the skater remounts and lands. Invented by Steve Olson, popularized in the 1980s.

name: Boneless One
history: A boneless where only one foot plants while the grab and re-mounting happen quickly. A specific variation of the boneless family of tricks.

name: Frontside Boneless
history: A boneless done with a frontside rotation. The skater plants and rotates frontside before landing.

name: One-Foot Ollie
history: An ollie performed with only one foot on the board, the other hanging free. A style trick demonstrating control and pop.
youtube_query: one foot ollie skateboard trick tutorial

name: Nollie 360 Backside Flip
history: A nollie backside 360 flip - combining nollie stance with the 360 flip rotation going backside. One of the rarest nollie combinations.

name: Fakie 360 Backside Flip
history: A backside 360 flip from fakie stance. The reverse riding adds a layer of complexity to the already demanding trick.

name: Switch Varial Kickflip
history: A varial kickflip in switch stance. The pop shove-it and kickflip are both executed with the non-dominant foot leading.

name: Nollie Laser Flip
history: A laser flip (frontside 360 heel) from nollie stance. Among the rarest and most technical nollie flip tricks.

name: Switch Laser Flip
history: A laser flip performed in switch stance. One of the most technical switch flip tricks known.

name: Fakie Big Flip
history: A big flip (backside 360 body rotation with kickflip) from fakie stance. An advanced fakie combination.

name: Nollie Big Flip
history: A big flip from nollie stance. A rare and technical nollie spin-flip combination.

name: Double Tre Flip
history: A 360 flip where the board completes two kickflip rotations instead of one during the 360 shove-it. Extremely rare and difficult.

name: Hospital Flip to Casper
history: A hospital flip that ends in a Casper stall position. A highly technical freestyle combo.

name: Nollie 360 Heelflip
history: A 360 heelflip from nollie stance. The board does a 360 frontside shove-it combined with a nollie heelflip.

name: Fakie 360 Heelflip
history: A 360 heelflip from fakie stance. Combines the reverse riding position with the demanding spin-flip combination.

name: 360 Heelflip
history: A 360-degree backside body rotation combined with a heelflip. The heelflip counterpart to the 360 kickflip.

name: Frontside 360 Kickflip
history: A frontside 360 body rotation combined with a kickflip. Distinct from the standard 360 flip by the frontside spin direction.

name: Frontside 360 Heelflip
history: A frontside 360 body rotation with a heelflip. A very rare combination combining two already demanding elements.

name: Bluntslide
history: The tail of the board slides on the obstacle surface while the back wheels hang over. A tail-first slide popular on ledges and rails.
youtube_query: how to blunt slide skateboard tutorial

name: Nosebluntslide
history: The nose of the board slides on the obstacle while the front wheels hang over the other side. The nose counterpart to the bluntslide.
youtube_query: how to nose blunt slide skateboard tutorial

name: Wallride
history: The skater rides up a wall using momentum and the curvature of their trajectory. A creative trick using vertical surfaces.

name: Wallride Nosegrind
history: A wallride that enters a nosegrind position on the top of a wall or rail. A creative combination of vertical and horizontal surfaces.

name: Stair Ollie
history: Ollying down a set of stairs. The number of stairs determines difficulty. A defining achievement in street skating since the late 1980s.

name: Kickflip Down Stairs
history: A kickflip over a stair set. The number of stairs and technical execution determine the trick's significance.

name: Heelflip Down Stairs
history: A heelflip over a stair set. Heelflips down large sets became landmark tricks in the video part era.

name: Gap Ollie
history: Ollying over a gap between two surfaces. Gap skating became central to street skating in the early 1990s.

name: Long Jump Ollie
history: An ollie focused on horizontal distance rather than height. Skateboarding's equivalent of the track-and-field long jump.
youtube_query: long jump ollie skateboard distance trick

name: Ollie Up
history: An ollie performed to land on a raised surface (curb, ledge, platform). The first gap technique skaters typically learn after basic flat-ground ollies.

name: Double Set Kickflip
history: A kickflip over a double stair set, a staircase with two separate landings. One of the most photogenic tricks in magazine-era skating.

name: Tre Flip Down Gap
history: A 360 flip over a gap or down stairs. One of the signature tricks of video-part era street skating.

name: Backside 180 Ollie Down Stairs
history: A backside 180 over a stair set. The rotation adds a level of commitment and technical difficulty to the stair gap.

name: Frontside 180 Ollie Down Stairs
history: A frontside 180 over a stair set. The frontside version requires different body mechanics and timing than the backside.

name: Nollie Flip Down Gap
history: A nollie kickflip over a gap. The nollie stance adds difficulty as the skater approaches and launches from the non-dominant foot.
youtube_query: nollie kickflip down gap stairs skateboard

name: Switch Flip Down Gap
history: A switch kickflip over a large gap or stairs. Considered among the most impressive gap tricks for its technical requirements.

name: Handplant Aerial (Pool)
history: Going above the lip of the pool while planting a hand on the coping briefly. Foundational to early pool skating in the 1970s.

name: Bert Slide
history: A deep carving slide where one hand drags on the bottom of the pool. Invented by Larry Bertlemann from surfing influence in the 1970s.
youtube_query: bert slide pool skateboard carving trick

name: Kickflip Indy
history: A kickflip combined with an Indy grab on a vert ramp. Mixing flip tricks with grabs is a hallmark of modern vert skating.
youtube_query: kickflip indy grab vert skateboard trick

name: Heelflip Melon
history: A heelflip combined with a melon grab on a vert ramp. A technical combination of a flip trick and a grab.
youtube_query: heelflip melon grab vert skateboard trick

name: Varial Heelflip to Tail
history: A varial heelflip that lands in a tailstall on the coping. Blends street-style flip tricks with transition skating.
youtube_query: varial heelflip tail stall vert skateboard

name: Noseblunt to Fakie (Ramp)
history: A noseblunt stall on the coping that exits fakie. A very technical ramp lip trick requiring precise nose placement.
youtube_query: nose blunt to fakie ramp skateboard tutorial

name: Cab Kickflip
history: A full Caballerial (fakie 360) combined with a kickflip on a vert ramp. Named using Cab's (Steve Caballero's) nickname combined with the kickflip.
